infobox.title: Insurance DApp
infobox.imageCaption: Ethereum smart contract interaction via MetaMask

lead:
The **Insurance DApp** is a decentralised insurance application built on the **Ethereum blockchain**, using Solidity smart contracts to replace the traditional insurance company as the intermediary. Policies, premiums, claims, and payouts are all handled by on-chain logic — eliminating the need for a centralised insurer to adjudicate claims.

The project was built to explore the gap between centralised insurance (where a company decides your claim) and decentralised insurance (where a smart contract executes pre-agreed rules automatically and transparently). It is part of a blockchain portfolio that also includes [[foss-coin|FOSS Coin]] — a live Solana SPL token.

content.content:
In traditional insurance, the insurer holds all the power: they collect premiums, evaluate claims, and decide payouts. A claimant has no ability to independently verify that the rules are being applied fairly.

A smart contract-based insurance system changes this: the rules are written in Solidity, deployed on-chain, and execute automatically when conditions are met. Anyone can read the contract code. Payouts happen without a human decision-maker in the loop.

The trade-off is that smart contracts cannot access real-world data without an oracle — and this DApp, built as a learning exercise, does not implement an oracle layer. It demonstrates the core mechanic of trustless policy execution rather than a production-ready insurance product.

content.title: Learning context
content.content: This project was built as a technical learning exercise. It demonstrates Solidity contract design, Truffle deployment workflow, and Web3.js/MetaMask browser integration — not as a live insurance product.

rows.Purpose: Policy logic, claim rules, payout execution on Ethereum
UI for policy purchase, claim submission, payout

content.content:
The DApp workflow follows a standard insurance lifecycle, but each step is enforced by the smart contract rather than a company employee:

1. **Policy purchase** — a user pays ETH to the contract, specifying the coverage terms. The contract records the policy on-chain.
2. **Premium lock** — the ETH is held in the contract's balance, not transferred to an insurer.
3. **Claim submission** — the user submits a claim via the web interface. The contract evaluates whether the claim meets the pre-coded conditions.
4. **Payout** — if the claim conditions are satisfied, the contract automatically transfers the payout amount from its balance to the claimant's wallet.
5. **Rejection** — if conditions are not met, the claim is rejected on-chain with no manual override possible.

All state transitions are recorded as on-chain events and verifiable by any block explorer.

items.caption: Policy purchase interface — MetaMask payment flow
Claim submission and smart contract evaluation
Automatic payout transaction on the Ethereum network

content.content:
The Insurance DApp and [[foss-coin|FOSS Coin]] represent the two main blockchain ecosystems worked with — Ethereum (EVM-based, Solidity smart contracts) and Solana (Rust-based runtime, SPL token program). They demonstrate breadth across different consensus mechanisms, programming models, and toolchains.

Both projects are listed in the [[about|GitHub repository table]] and form the blockchain/Web3 component of the technical skills section.
